identity.name: Nikolaos Nikolaou
identity.tagline: Senior XR Developer · Tabletop DM · Maker
identity.classChain: Senior XR Developer 6 / Tabletop DM 7 / Maker 5 / Painter 3
identity.bio: Senior Unity gameplay programmer with 6+ years of high-performance C# architecture and polished player interactions. Master of strict VR/XR technical requirements — spatial UI, 3D math, optimization for mobile. I build modular, decoupled systems that support rapid iteration and large-scale content delivery. I turn complex requirements into intuitive, responsive gameplay. Self-driven collaborator committed to technical excellence and meticulous refinement of the player experience.
identity.adventurerIntro: Builds gameplay systems for a living, runs tabletop campaigns for the love of it, and finishes Grey Knights one model at a time. He'll out-argue you about three things at once and somehow still ship the fourth.
identity.backstory: Greek by birth, Copenhagen by choice. Currently building a TTRPG system on the side. Probably has more hobbies than fingers.
howToPlay: Click stats, skills, factions — most things react.
Seven secrets are hidden across the site. The 🔍 counter keeps score.
Turn on voice for narrator commentary — toggle in the corner. Text always shows.
Anything labeled "do not click" is, of course, asking to be clicked.

stats.label: XR Architecture
stats.blurb: Strict VR optimization, spatial UI, mobile XR.

stats.label: Systems Thinking
stats.blurb: Sees the second-order effects before the first ones ship.

stats.label: Adversarial CHA
stats.blurb: Will argue against a closed-and-done conclusion if it makes the conversation better.

stats.label: Caffeine Resistance
stats.blurb: Drinks coffee anyway. Calls it a superpower.

stats.label: Painting
stats.blurb: Resin and FDM. Minis, statues, conversions.

stats.label: Fitness
stats.blurb: The character has never invested a point here.

traits.name: Hyperfocus
traits.desc: Locks onto a problem until it's solved. Forgets meals. Worth it.

traits.name: Break-and-Repurpose
traits.desc: Uses tools against their design. Often it works. Sometimes it works better.

traits.name: Think Fast
traits.desc: Reasons through what'll work before building. Rejects 'fail fast' as wasteful.

traits.name: Anticipate Failure
traits.desc: Names the failure modes before they fail. The future thanks him.

traits.name: Adversarial Communicator
traits.desc: Defends the non-obvious. Challenges the consensus. Makes meetings worth having.

traits.name: ADHD-Probably
traits.desc: Multiple hobbies, multiple obsessions, one extremely tidy git history.

languages.name: Corporate Jargon
languages.level: Passable — can translate 'synergy' into an actual action item

languages.name: Technical Jargon
languages.level: Fluent — speaks Engineer and Stakeholder, often in the same sentence

languages.name: Manager Talk
languages.level: Conversational — knows when 'let's circle back' means 'never'

bullets: Designed custom Unity tools that accelerated prototyping and shortened design-feedback loops.
Led high-fidelity training simulations with strict modular architecture and performance budgets.
Owned performance profiling and debugging for heavy scenes — kept everything smooth.
Mentored junior developers and reviewed code to raise team production standards.
Spearheaded engineering on an SXSW-award-winning project — translated abstract design into C# mechanics.
Built development templates and standardized assets that streamlined studio workflow.
Worked tight loops with artists and designers — bridged visual fidelity and technical constraints.
Developed on-location AR with high-precision tracking and custom interaction logic.
Built interactive training simulations for healthcare education in Denmark and East Africa.
Sole programmer on a narrative game demo in Unreal 5 — owned gameplay systems concept to DFI-funded vertical slice.
Implemented character controllers, interaction systems, game states — responsive, bug-free demo.
Collaborated on core design decisions — mechanics had to be fun, responsive, technically feasible.
Applied Agile to align technical deliverables with evolving design goals.

education.thesis: Comfort XR — investigating immersion systems to alleviate physical discomfort.

education.degree: MSc Products and Systems Design Engineering
education.thesis: Big Chimera — location-based serious AR game.

factions.name: Rohan
factions.blurb: Horse lords. He likes that the cool guys ride in late and turn the battle.

factions.name: Grey Knights
factions.blurb: Daemon hunters in silver. He paints them one model at a time. He'll finish. Probably.

factions.name: Planeswalkers
factions.blurb: 100-card singleton format. Long games, weird boards, longer arguments about politics.

library.booksNote: The character will not be taking that proficiency.

games.title: The Beginner's Guide
games.note: We're basically inside it right now.

games.title: The Stanley Parable
games.note: Sets the tone for this entire site.

showcases.title: My TTRPG
showcases.story: A custom TTRPG system, born from years of DM-ing D&D and a homebrew Faerûn that kept outgrowing its borrowed bones. Rules, setting, and stories — built together so the mechanics serve the world and the world earns its mechanics. Currently in active development.
milestones: Custom Faerûn that kept outgrowing canon
Started building my own system in 2024
Rules and setting growing in parallel

showcases.title: 3D Print & Paint
showcases.story: Started with one printer. Ended up with a small company for a while. Resin for fine detail, FDM for terrain and brackets. I paint the minis I print — Grey Knights for 40k, Rohan for Middle-earth Strategy Battle Game, and one-off characters for whoever's at the table.

showcases.title: Homebrew Faerûn
showcases.tagline: Six years of DM notes — the players' fault, mostly
showcases.story: A Faerûn that's been edited by the players for six years. I run a mix of homebrew arcs and heavily-modified prebuilt campaigns — modified to what my players actually want to do. Rules and story beats are improvised when the table demands it. Canon doesn't survive contact with the party.

showcases.title: Khora — XR Work
showcases.story: Spearheaded engineering on the studio's award-winning XR work — including an SXSW-recognized project. Built on-location AR with high-precision tracking, custom interaction systems for VR, and interactive training simulations for healthcare in Denmark and East Africa. Bridged visual fidelity with technical constraints in tight loops with artists and designers.
milestones: Studio-wide dev templates and asset standards
